papers.title_en: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
papers.authors: Zanyi Wang, Xin Lin, Haodong Li, Dengyang Jiang, Yijiang Li
papers.evidence: 直接处理alpha抠图预测作为密集场读出，与alpha通道生成相关
papers.tldr: 文本到图像模型预训练富含语义、结构和几何先验，但现有密集预测方法将其视为目标生成，需解码器生成RGB风格输出，造成开销和偏差。本文提出ReChannel，利用预训练DiT的patch-to-token-to-patch网格结构，每个token直接映射到固定输出patch的任务原生场，仅用约33K参数的共享线性头。在六个密集预测任务上，无trimap抠图、KITTI深度和指代分割达到新SOTA，法线、显著性、姿态具竞争力；在4B参数量下比编辑+潜解码方法准确率更高且快2.48倍。表明密集预测可从生成式预训练受益，无需继承其输出接口。
papers.abstract_en: Large-scale text-to-image models are attractive backbones for dense prediction because RGB generation pretraining learns rich semantic, structural, and geometric priors. Existing generative and editing approaches reuse these priors by casting dense prediction as target generation: annotations such as depth, normals, alpha mattes, masks, and heatmaps are encoded into an RGB-trained VAE latent space and decoded back as image-like targets. We argue this inherits more of the generative output interface than dense prediction requires: unlike RGB synthesis, dense prediction asks for pixel-correct, task-native fields on the same image plane, not new RGB content to be rendered. Our key observation is that a pretrained DiT already organizes RGB inputs through a patch-to-token-to-patch lattice on the image plane, so each token indexes a fixed output patch whose channels can carry task-native quantities instead of RGB appearance. We instantiate this as ReChannel: we keep the VAE encoder for the DiT's input distribution but drop the target-side decoder, adapt the frozen DiT with task LoRA, and map each token to its p x p x K_t pixel-space patch through a shared token-local linear head--about 33K parameters, no spatial mixing. Using FLUX-Klein, we evaluate on six dense prediction tasks and over a dozen benchmarks. This minimal interface sets new state-of-the-art on trimap-free matting, KITTI depth, and referring segmentation, and stays competitive on normals, saliency, and pose. In a matched 4B setting it is more accurate and 2.48x faster than an edit-plus-latent-decode counterpart--dense perception can benefit from generative pretraining without inheriting its output interface.
